panel: coupling v4 estimator-admissibility gate -- pre-freeze panel #7

lens_reviews.summary: The gate's insertion point is genuinely one-sided-proof -- every return before line 574 is a VOID, so no finding branch escapes it -- but S1's pool construction is broken in two structural ways and its coverage is incomplete in a third. The pseudo-pool is sign-antisymmetric by construction, which makes the specificity leg (S3) easier to pass on the pseudo-population than on the real one by an artifact (~0.158) larger than the threshold it is tested against -- the same tautology signature that voided the certifier itself. The pool also samples only the FIXED arm, so it never contains the accumulate arm's noise, which is where the real estimator's variance actually lives. And the gate covers only the raw estimator: the `NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded` branch -- which the prereg calls "a stronger, more precise statement than the bounded null" -- is decided by the guard-excluded estimator's silence, an estimator the prereg itself already admits is downward-biased and which S1 never tests. That is attempt-3's defect surviving the fix on an adjacent channel.

findings.title: The guard-excluded estimator is never gated, yet its silence returns a favourable headline
findings.claim: S1 builds the null pool from `fixed_i.gen_aggregate` only. But `compute_verdict` runs TWO estimators, and the second one -- the guard-excluded channel -- solely decides the `NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B` branch by failing to be COUPLED. A deaf guard-excluded estimator therefore converts every genuine COUPLED into a favourable 'the price was style, not capability' claim, and the proposed gate does not touch it.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:564 builds `dr_ge = dose_response(..., battery_key="battery_gen_guard_excl")`; :574-578 `if dr["coupled"]: if not dr_ge["coupled"]: return "NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B"` -- the branch fires on dr_ge's SILENCE, never on a positive dr_ge finding. The bias direction is not speculative: capability_battery_gen.py:319-321 computes `n_scored = n - (rep + echo)` and `acc_guard_excl = correct / n_scored`, so the guard-excluded denominator SHRINKS with guard fires and is itself dose-dependent (the accumulate arm guards more as it degrades) -- strictly coarser and noisier than the raw channel. PREREG_B2:153-157 states the string is "a stronger, more precise statement than the bounded null" AND admits "the guard-excluded slopes are DOWNWARD-BIASED when the accumulate arm guards more than the fixed arm." So the program has already conceded that the estimator deciding its strongest favourable claim is biased toward silence, and S1 leaves it uncertified. The fields needed exist at result time: points carries both `gen_aggregate` and `gen_aggregate_guard_excl` (:192-193).
findings.fix: Run the estimator gate ONCE PER battery_key, drawing the pool from the matching points field (`gen_aggregate` for dr, `gen_aggregate_guard_excl` for dr_ge). Require BOTH legs on BOTH channels. A failed guard-excluded sensitivity leg must VOID (`VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive__guard_excl`) -- it must never be allowed to fall through into `NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B`, because that branch is precisely the claim its deafness manufactures. Report both channels' recovery/false-recovery rates side by side in the receipt.

findings.title: The pseudo-pool is sign-antisymmetric, so the specificity leg is structurally easier than the real population
findings.claim: S1 forms ORDERED pairs (i,j), i != j. Then d_ji = -d_ij exactly, so slope_ji = -slope_ij exactly. The frozen COUPLED rule requires `len(neg_above) == 0`. Therefore ANY pseudo-run containing both members of a mirror pair is structurally incapable of returning COUPLED, regardless of the data. This deflates false_recovery_rate by an artifact of the same magnitude as the threshold it is compared against -- S3 passes for reasons that have nothing to do with the estimator.
findings.evidence: Arithmetic on the frozen rule at coupling_confirm_v4.py:421-424: `pos_above = [s for s in slopes if s >= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE]`, `neg_above = [s for s in slopes if s <= -MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE]`, `coupled = len(pos_above) > n_admissible/2 and len(neg_above) == 0`. Given a mirror pair {s, -s} in a 3-pseudo-seed run: if |s| >= bar then neg_above >= 1 -> not COUPLED; if |s| < bar then at most 1 of 3 can reach +bar, failing the strict majority -> not COUPLED. Either way COUPLED is unreachable. Draw rate: 5 seeds -> 20 ordered pairs -> 10 mirror pairs; 3-subsets containing a mirror pair = 10 * 18 = 180 out of C(20,3) = 1140 = 0.158 -- larger than the proposed ESTIMATOR_FALSE_RECOVERY_MAX = 0.15. Worse, index-disjointness is impossible: 3 disjoint pairs require 6 distinct seeds and only 5 exist, so every pseudo-run of size n_admissible >= 3 MUST reuse a fixed-arm trajectory. The real 5 seeds are independent draws; the pseudo-seeds are not. S1's claim that this is 'EXACTLY the population the real estimator fits' is false on the dependence dimension, and the dependence biases BOTH legs toward passing (shared trajectories correlate pseudo-slopes, inflating the 'all above bar' rate under injection in S2).
findings.fix: Abandon pair-differencing as the pool. Build the null from the verdict's own residuals instead: per dose-admissible seed, fit the real span-[2,8] line to that seed's own paired deltas, take the residuals, and resample/sign-flip them against the real rank axis to synthesize pseudo-seeds. This preserves independence across seeds, uses the real two-arm contrast, and is still zero-GPU (the data is already in `per_seed[seed]["deltas"]`/`fit_ranks`, :404-407). If pair-differencing is kept anyway, restrict to the 10 UNORDERED pairs, forbid any pseudo-run from containing two pairs sharing a seed index, and report the realized fraction of draws that are structurally COUPLED-incapable -- if that fraction is non-zero, the specificity leg is void by construction.

findings.title: The pool omits the accumulate arm, so recovery_rate is measured at the wrong noise level
findings.claim: The verdict's statistic is a WITHIN-seed, CROSS-arm contrast (fixed - accumulate). S1's pool is a CROSS-seed, WITHIN-arm contrast (fixed_i - fixed_j) that contains zero accumulate-arm variance. The accumulate arm is the one that actually moves under dose, degrades, and accrues guard fires; the fixed arm is dose-pinned and quiescent. If the pinned arm's cross-seed spread is smaller than the real paired-delta fit residual, recovery_rate OVERSTATES the estimator's power, the gate passes, and `NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B` is stamped 'estimator-validated' by an estimator never tested at its own noise level. That is exactly the branch this gate exists to earn.
findings.evidence: The real statistic: coupling_confirm_v4.py:398 `d = CBG.paired_delta(fix_p[battery_key], acc_p[battery_key], selected)` -- fixed minus accumulate, same seed, matched step. The pseudo statistic per S1 uses only `fixed_*.gen_aggregate`. The two arms are not symmetric sources of noise: the fixed arm sets `Uacc[L] = subs_now[L]` each refit (:691-692) so its rank never grows, while the accumulate arm takes `orthonormal_union` (:689) and its rank climbs 2->24, which is the whole dose axis. Guard fires -- the dominant discretization noise in the aggregate (capability_battery_gen.py:319-321) -- grow on the accumulate arm and are why `guard_fire_totals` exists per-arm at :200-214. The spec provides no check that the pool's dispersion matches the real estimator's, and the direction of the mismatch is unverified in the very direction that licenses a favourable branch.
findings.fix: Same remedy as F2: derive the pool from detrended REAL paired deltas so the accumulate arm's variance is in the pool by construction. Independently, add a disclosure-and-gate: compute the pseudo-pool's residual SD and the real per-seed fit residual SD from data already on hand, report both in the receipt, and VOID (`VOID_COUPLING__estimator_null_understates_noise`) if the pool's residual SD is materially below the real one -- an estimator gate calibrated on quieter data than it certifies is not a gate.

findings.title: Only the COUPLED rule is exercised; the bounded-null and PARTIAL branches inherit their license by implication
findings.claim: Both legs measure 'fraction of pseudo-runs returning COUPLED.' But the verdict has three finding branches, and the two the gate is meant to earn -- the bounded null and PARTIAL -- have their own decision rules that are never run against the pseudo-population. The bounded null's actual false-claim rate (P[bounded_null | a true bar-sized effect]) is bounded only implicitly at <= 1 - recovery_rate = 0.20, and is never computed or reported. PARTIAL is not protected at all.
findings.evidence: The three branches are distinct rules: `coupled` at coupling_confirm_v4.py:424, `bounded_null = len(below) > n_admissible/2` at :425, and the residual PARTIAL fall-through at :588. They are not complementary -- a pseudo-run can be none of COUPLED/bounded_null. S2/S3 as written return only the COUPLED indicator, so under bar-sized injection the split of the remaining ~20% between 'bounded null' (a favourable false claim) and 'PARTIAL' (an honest non-claim) is unmeasured. The paper's negative result would carry no stated error rate for its own branch.
findings.fix: Have each pseudo-run return the full verdict branch, not a boolean. Record and report, at injection 0 and injection = bar, all three rates: coupled_rate, bounded_null_rate, partial_rate. Gate the bounded null on its OWN number (ESTIMATOR_FALSE_BOUNDED_NULL_MAX, pre-committed), and publish `bounded_null_rate under injection = bar` as the disclosed error rate of the negative claim. A negative result whose own miss rate is unmeasured is not earned by a gate that only measured the positive rule.

findings.title: VOID is retryable and the program's incentive points squarely at it
findings.claim: S4 makes either leg's failure VOID the entire run. Nothing in S1-S4 or the prereg pre-commits what a VOID means for the ATTEMPT. Since a VOID leaves the standing PARTIAL untouched and records no disconfirming result, it is strictly the cheapest outcome for a program whose paper is blocked on this branch -- and it licenses 'adjust the estimator, re-run,' which is the garden of forking paths the whole arc exists to prevent.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2:76-80 pre-commits that gate failure is 'a VOID, never a finding' but says nothing about whether a VOID terminates the attempt or whether VOIDed runs are disclosed. Arc history in the two panel documents: six pre-freeze kills, the paper blocked on coupling, and the standing fallback is PARTIAL -- so a VOID costs the program nothing relative to a disconfirming bounded null or an honest PARTIAL, while a successful re-tune costs it nothing either. The estimator gate is the first gate in this harness whose failure is plausibly fixable by editing the estimator rather than by re-running the model, which is precisely what makes it tempting.
findings.fix: Pre-commit in the prereg, before freeze: `VOID_COUPLING__estimator_*` is TERMINAL for this prereg. Any successor attempt requires a new frozen prereg and a new pre-freeze panel, and the paper must disclose every VOIDed run with its receipt path and its realized recovery/false-recovery rates. Without this clause the gate converts a hard question into an optional one.

findings.title: ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR = 0.80 is asserted, and neither new constant nor either new verdict string is wired into the frozen surfaces
findings.claim: 0.15 at least mirrors MAX_FIRE and inherits its provenance; 0.80 has none stated. More concretely, the spec's two constants and two verdict strings are absent from the two structures that make a constant frozen and a verdict auditable in this harness, so as written the gate's own numbers would not appear in the receipt and its verdicts would not be in the frozen set.
findings.evidence: `_thresholds()` at coupling_confirm_v4.py:591-603 enumerates every gating constant and is what lands in the receipt (`"thresholds": _thresholds()` at :905) -- ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR and ESTIMATOR_FALSE_RECOVERY_MAX are not in it. `FROZEN_VERDICTS` at :130-144 does not contain `VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive` or `VOID_COUPLING__estimator_nonspecific`, and the hygiene check `checks["dry_verdicts_all_frozen"]` at :1250-1252 only tests verdicts that a --dry case actually produced -- so if no --dry case exercises the new strings, the check passes vacuously and the omission ships silently.
findings.fix: Add both constants to `_thresholds()` and both strings to `FROZEN_VERDICTS`; add --dry fixtures that actually emit each new string so the frozen-set check is non-vacuous. In the prereg, state 0.80's arithmetic in terms of what it buys the reader: 'recovery >= 0.80 at the bar bounds the bounded null's own miss rate at the bar at <= 0.20, which is disclosed as the negative claim's error rate.' A floor whose consequence is not stated is a number the panel cannot audit.

findings.title: Pseudo-run denominator and rank-axis provenance are underspecified -- the panel-#6 fatal can recur inside the gate
findings.claim: `_per_seed_slope` returns None whenever a unit has fewer than 2 distinct fitted ranks. Pseudo-seeds can stall the same way real seeds can. The spec never says what the pseudo-run's majority denominator is, nor how a None pseudo-slope counts -- which is the exact defect that killed v4 at panel #6, now reproduced one level up inside the instrument that is supposed to prevent it. S1 also says the rank axis comes from 'the accumulate arm's matched-step schedule' without naming WHOSE accumulate arm for a cross-seed pair.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:355-358 `_per_seed_slope` returns None on `len(set(ranks)) < 2`. The real rule was hardened at :419-424 to use `n_admissible = len(admissible_seeds)` as the denominator, with the comment at :413-418 spelling out that dropping None-slopes from the denominator lets '4 stalled + 1 above-bar' read a strict majority from ONE seed. S1 inherits none of this hardening. Rank schedules are seed-dependent because rank growth comes from `B2COUP.orthonormal_union(Uacc[L], subs_now[L])` at :689 -- how much new direction each refit contributes is a property of that seed's trajectory -- so seeds i and j need not share a rank axis over the span [2,8].
findings.fix: Pre-commit that the pseudo-run applies the frozen rule with the SAME denominator convention: denominator = the pseudo-run's pseudo-seed count, None counting against the majority; and that a pseudo-run with fewer than MIN_ADMISSIBLE_SEEDS defined slopes is discarded and re-drawn (with the discard rate reported -- a high discard rate is itself evidence the estimator is stalling). Name the rank axis explicitly: use the accumulate schedule of the FIRST index of the pair, restricted to steps present in both members. Assert both in --dry with a stalled-pseudo-seed fixture, mirroring `stalled_seeds_never_coupled` at :1132-1140.

findings.title: The insertion point is one-sided-proof but unpinned by any test
findings.claim: On mandate (b): the ordering is genuinely clean. Every return that precedes the proposed insertion point is a VOID that asserts nothing -- no_bite, underpowered, the four battery-gate VOIDs, and the reduced-slope-count underpowered -- so no finding can escape the gate PROVIDED it is inserted between the underpowered check and the `if dr["coupled"]` block. Nothing in S1-S4 or the test suite pins that placement, so a later edit could silently move a finding in front of it.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:555 (`no_bite`), :559 (`underpowered`), :562 (gate VOIDs), :573 (`underpowered` on slope count) are all VOID returns; the first claim-bearing return is :578 (`NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B`), followed by :584 (PARTIAL via FI), :585 (COUPLED), :587 (bounded null), :588 (PARTIAL). Inserting the estimator gate between :573 and :574 places all five claim-bearing returns behind it. No existing --dry case would fail if it were placed after :578.
findings.fix: Add four --dry cases that hold the curves fixed and force the estimator legs to fail, asserting that each of the four claim-bearing fixtures (`coupled_dose_slope`, `echo_subtractive_downgrade`, `fi_dose_downgrade`, `bounded_null`, `partial_sign_split`) converts to the estimator VOID string. That pins the placement as a behavioural invariant rather than a line number, in the style of `stalled_seeds_never_coupled`.

lens_reviews.lens: exchangeability / noise realism of the cross-seed fixed-vs-fixed pseudo-delta null pool (S1-S4)
lens_reviews.summary: The pool is not a faithful null for the real estimator, and -- decisively -- both legs of the proposed gate are broken by arithmetic before any data exists. Because OLS slope is linear, injecting exactly MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE puts every pseudo-seed at an exact coin flip, capping recovery_rate at 0.5 for ANY non-degenerate symmetric pool (the 0.80 floor is reachable only if >=34% of pseudo-seeds have exactly-zero slope, i.e. only by a pool that reports no noise -- attempt-3's defect one level up); and the ordered-pair pool is sign-antisymmetric by construction, so the COUPLED rule's zero-negatives clause caps false_recovery at 0.066 < 0.15, making S3 mathematically unfailable. Separately, the real estimator's rank-4 point is a structural zero (the two arms are bit-identical through step 24), so the real per-seed slope is d(rank8)/4 -- a one-checkpoint, zero-anchored, 1/48-lattice statistic that the free-floating continuous pseudo-slope cannot be exchangeable with. Fix direction: anchor and re-scale the pool to the realized paired design, replace the fixed recovery floor with a measured MDE sweep whose value must be carried in the bounded-null string, and delete the specificity leg rather than ship a leg that cannot fire.

findings.title: S2 sensitivity leg: recovery_rate is capped at 0.5 by construction; the only way to pass is a degenerate (no-noise) pool
findings.claim: Injecting a slope of exactly MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE and then testing 'slope >= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE' is a coin flip per pseudo-seed, so ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR=0.80 is unreachable for any non-degenerate pool; the gate therefore VOIDs the overnight run with probability ~1, and the only pools that CAN pass it are ones with a large atom at exactly-zero slope -- i.e. the leg selects for a null that reports no noise.
findings.evidence: OLS slope is linear in the response: `_per_seed_slope` is `np.polyfit(ranks, deltas, 1)[0]` (coupling_confirm_v4.py:355-358), so slope(d + c*r) = slope(d) + c exactly. With c = MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.0152 (coupling_confirm_v4.py:107), P(injected pseudo-slope >= bar) = P(null pseudo-slope >= 0) = 0.5 for a continuous symmetric pool. The frozen COUPLED rule needs a strict majority (coupling_confirm_v4.py:421-425: `len(pos_above) > n_admissible/2 and len(neg_above)==0`), so recovery_rate <= P(Binom(5,0.5)>=3) = 0.5 exactly, before the zero-negatives clause subtracts more. 0.5 < 0.80. To reach 0.80 you need per-seed P >= ~0.67, i.e. P(null slope >= 0) = 0.5 + a/2 >= 0.67, i.e. an atom of a >= 0.34 at exactly zero slope. That is the attempt-3 mechanism (a deaf instrument passes the floor best) relocated from the battery to the null pool.
findings.fix: Do not test a fixed floor at a single injected slope. Sweep the injected slope over a pre-committed grid (e.g. 0.5x to 4x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE) and REPORT s*, the smallest injected slope at which recovery reaches 0.80 -- the estimator's actual MDE. Then pre-commit a verdict-mapping consequence rather than a VOID: the bounded-null string may only be emitted carrying s* in it (the bound is s*, not 0.0152), and if s* > k x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE for a pre-frozen k the bounded-null branch is replaced by VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive. This converts an unsatisfiable gate into the measurement the arc actually needs.

findings.title: S3 specificity leg is unfailable: an ordered-pair pool is sign-antisymmetric, capping false_recovery at 0.066 < 0.15
findings.claim: The false-recovery leg cannot fail for any sign-symmetric pool, so it certifies nothing -- the tautological-leg signature that already voided this program's own certifier -- and its outcome is anyway controlled by an unspecified draw rule rather than by the estimator.
findings.evidence: S1 forms d_ij over ORDERED pairs i != j, so the pool is closed under negation (d_ji = -d_ij) and its slope distribution is exactly symmetric about 0. Under symmetry let p = P(slope >= bar) = P(slope <= -bar) and m = 1-2p. The frozen rule (coupling_confirm_v4.py:421-425) gives P(COUPLED) = 10p^3*m^2 + 5p^4*m + p^5, whose maximum over p in [0,0.5] is ~0.0662 (at p ~ 0.355; p=0.5 gives 1/32 = 0.031). ESTIMATOR_FALSE_RECOVERY_MAX = 0.15 sits above the theoretical maximum of the statistic. The only way to exceed 0.15 is positive sign-correlation between drawn pseudo-seeds -- which arises purely from draws sharing a common trajectory (e.g. (0,1),(0,2),(0,3),(0,4)) -- and S1 does not specify the draw rule, so the leg's verdict is decided by an unpinned implementation detail, not by the estimator.
findings.fix: Delete S3 and stop calling the gate two-sided on this pool. Report false_recovery_rate as a non-gating descriptive WITH its theoretical maximum (0.066) printed beside it so no reader mistakes the pass for evidence, and state plainly in the prereg that estimator specificity is not certifiable without a paired arm carrying a KNOWN-ZERO price at non-constant dose -- the positive control the prereg already names as the program's open problem (PREREG lines 96-98). Removing a leg that cannot fire is the honest move; keeping it re-creates the fire_threshold tautology that voided styxx.instrument_admissibility's default specificity leg.

findings.title: The real estimator's rank-4 point is a structural zero the pool does not have: the real slope is d(rank8)/4, a one-checkpoint anchored lattice statistic
findings.claim: Both arms of a seed are bit-identical through step 24, so the paired delta at the first post-refit checkpoint (rank ~4) is exactly 0 by construction; the real per-seed slope collapses to d(rank8)/4 with zero leverage on the middle point, while every pseudo-seed carries a free, nonzero, continuous value at rank 4. The two statistics have different support, different dimensionality and different distributional shape -- the pool is non-exchangeable with the estimator it claims to certify, so BOTH rates are computed on a distribution the real estimator can never produce.
findings.evidence: ARMS (coupling_confirm_v4.py:87) gives accumulate and fixed the SAME lam=1.0; they differ only inside the refit branch (coupling_confirm_v4.py:683-698). Both arms of a seed share init and data order (`torch.manual_seed(seed); np.random.seed(seed)` at :623, `rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)` at :635, lora_dropout=0.0 at :625). The refit branch first executes at step == REFIT_EVERY == 25, and `audit(step)` is appended immediately after (:698) -- i.e. after 25 IDENTICAL optimizer steps. Hence gen_aggregate(fixed_s, 25) == gen_aggregate(acc_s, 25) and paired_delta == 0.0000. The fit filter excludes only step 0 (`if st > 0 and lo <= r <= hi`, :401), so this second structural zero IS in the fit at rank ~4. On the prereg's own realized grid {4,6,8} (PREREG lines 346-349), x_bar=6, Sxx=8, so slope = (-2*d4 + 0*d6 + 2*d8)/8 = (d8 - d4)/4 = d8/4. The middle checkpoint has exactly zero leverage. Additionally the aggregate is a mean of k>=3 sub-task scores over 16 items each (capability_battery_gen.py:460 asserts count in (8,16)), so with 3 selected the delta lives on a 1/48 = 0.020833 lattice and the real slope lives on a 0.00521 lattice: 'slope >= 0.0152' is literally 'the accumulate arm got at least 3 of 48 items more wrong at rank 8'. The injected continuous pseudo-slope has no such lattice.
findings.fix: Three coupled changes, all pre-freeze. (1) Extend ruling (c): exclude the first post-refit checkpoint as a structural zero the same way step 0 is excluded, and state in the prereg that the span-[2,8] fit then has at most TWO informative points (the '3 fitted points, slope SE 0.036' arithmetic at PREREG 346-349 is wrong as written). (2) If the anchor is KEPT, the pool must be anchored identically: replace d~_ij(r) with d~_ij(r) - d~_ij(r_first), so pseudo-seeds start from a forced zero at the same rank. (3) Quantize the injected pseudo-deltas onto the realized 1/(16*len(selected)) lattice before fitting, so the recovery rate is measured on the estimator's actual support.

findings.title: Variance scale: the pool is a null for the UNPAIRED estimator; it re-imports exactly the seed variance the paired design was built to remove
findings.claim: Var(fixed_i - fixed_j) is an independent-runs variance (different LoRA init, different data order, divergence from initialization); Var(fixed_s - acc_s) over ranks [2,8] is a same-run, short-horizon, common-ancestor variance (divergence for 25-50 steps from a bit-identical model under a rank-2-vs-rank-N erasure target). The pool overstates the real estimator's noise, plausibly by a large factor, and the direction compounds E1: an overstated null makes the sensitivity leg harder still and pushes the run to VOID regardless of the science.
findings.evidence: Per-seed identity of init and batch order across arms: coupling_confirm_v4.py:623, :635; only the refit branch differs (:683-698). The prereg itself states the paired design's purpose -- 'fixed-arm ... minus accumulate-arm ... at the MATCHED step, same seed (shared fine-tuning drift differences out; attempt 2's disease)' (PREREG lines 106-107). The pseudo-pair shares nothing but the base model and subs0 (computed once on the base, coupling_confirm_v4.py:754 and threaded to all seeds), so its delta carries the full seed-level component. Note also that greedy decode on a FIXED 48-item bank (capability_battery_gen.py:313-317) means item idiosyncrasy is a shared constant that cancels in the real paired delta; the quoted per-checkpoint binomial SE of 0.0722 (coupling_confirm_v4.py:123) is therefore NOT the real delta's SE and cannot be used to argue the pool's scale is right.
findings.fix: Do not assert scale -- MEASURE it and gate on the measurement. Compute and report ratio = SD(pseudo-deltas at matched rank) / SD(realized paired deltas at matched rank), and pre-commit that the estimator gate's rates are only interpretable when the ratio falls inside a frozen band (e.g. [0.5, 2.0]); outside it, report the rates as uninterpretable rather than as a certificate. The only faithful alternative is a second constant-dose arm per seed (same pin, different nuisance draw) giving a WITHIN-seed same-dose contrast -- that is GPU cost, and if it is not paid the spec must say the pool is a scale-mismatched surrogate.

findings.title: The 20 ordered pseudo-seeds are not independent: each seed appears in 8 of them, and the pool contains exact +/- twins that force non-COUPLED
findings.claim: Unconstrained draws of 5 from 20 mix perfectly anti-correlated twins and trajectory-sharing pairs, so both reported rates are artifacts of the draw rule rather than properties of the estimator, and the reported rates carry an undisclosed Monte-Carlo variance.
findings.evidence: S1's ordered construction yields 5*4 = 20 pseudo-seeds; each fixed trajectory appears in 4 as minuend and 4 as subtrahend. A draw containing both (i,j) and (j,i) has slopes s and -s: whenever |s| >= bar it guarantees one seed at or below -bar, and the frozen rule `len(neg_above)==0` (coupling_confirm_v4.py:424) forces non-COUPLED -- driving false_recovery down for a reason that has nothing to do with the estimator. A draw containing (i,j),(i,k),(i,l),(i,m) is dominated by seed i's single trajectory, collapsing five 'seeds' to roughly one, which inflates sign-consistency in both directions. The real run's 5 seeds are genuinely independent; the pseudo-runs are not, so the draws are not exchangeable with the population the rule is applied to.
findings.fix: Pre-commit the draw as a directed 5-cycle over the seeds (0->1->2->3->4->0 and its relabelings): each seed appears exactly once as minuend and once as subtrahend, no +/- twins, no shared-minuend domination. State in the prereg that only 24 directed 5-cycles exist (12 up to reversal), so the achievable resolution on a rate is 1/24 -- and that a 0.80 floor on 24 draws has a binomial SE of ~0.08, which must be disclosed with the rate or the floor is finer than the measurement.

findings.title: The rank axis borrowed by the pool is unspecified and does not reproduce the realized per-seed fit design
findings.claim: S1 says r(step) comes from 'the accumulate arm's matched-step schedule' but a pseudo-seed (i,j) has two candidate schedules and the spec picks neither; worse, even once pinned, the borrowed schedule gives the pseudo-seed a different design matrix (which steps land in [2,8], how many, at what x) than the seed it stands in for.
findings.evidence: erased_rank is recomputed per checkpoint from the arm's own accumulated subspace (coupling_confirm_v4.py:666) and grows only on the accumulate arm via `orthonormal_union` (:689), so the schedule is seed-specific and can stall (the harness has a dedicated stalled-seed fixture, :959-963, and a stalled-seed underpowered guard, :572). Fixed-arm checkpoints all carry erased_rank == 2, so the axis is entirely borrowed. The in-span filter is `lo <= r <= hi` (:401), so a fast-accumulating seed contributes fewer fitted points than a slow one -- and the harness already records the realized design per seed (`n_fit_pairs`, `fit_ranks`, :404-406), which the pool ignores.
findings.fix: Pin it in the prereg: pseudo-seed (i,j) borrows acc_i's schedule (minuend's seed), stated explicitly. Then resample the REALIZED designs rather than a nominal one -- for each pseudo-run, assign the 5 realized (fit_ranks, n_fit_pairs) designs from `dose_response`'s own per_seed receipt to the 5 pseudo-seeds, so the pool's leverage structure equals the run's.

findings.title: Training drift: the differential component inflates the pool, the common-mode component cancels -- and common-mode is the one that manufactures COUPLED
findings.claim: The fixed arm is still training at constant dose, so fixed_i - fixed_j carries differential drift that maps straight onto the borrowed rank axis (rank is monotone in step) and inflates pool variance; meanwhile any drift COMMON to all constant-dose arms cancels exactly by antisymmetry, so the pool's mean is identically zero at every rank. The nuisance that would actually fabricate a COUPLED verdict -- 'more erased directions penalized => more distortion at the same step', a common-mode positive slope in the real fixed-minus-accumulate contrast that is not a price on the honesty read specifically -- is structurally absent from the pool, so no leg built on it can ever detect it.
findings.evidence: The fixed arm trains for the full STEPS=300 with ERASE_ALPHA=4.0 and lam=1.0 (coupling_confirm_v4.py:87, :93, :95) and merely re-derives its rank-2 subspace at each refit (:690-692); it is constant-DOSE, not constant-MODEL. Rank on the accumulate arm is monotone in step (:689, :666), so step-indexed drift is collinear with the borrowed dose axis by construction. Antisymmetry of the ordered-pair pool (S1) forces E[d~_ij(r)] = 0 at every r, whatever the common drift.
findings.fix: State in the prereg, as a named residual, that this pool bounds only ZERO-MEAN nuisance and is blind to common-mode dose-collinear nuisance -- and that this blindness is the same gap the missing paired positive control leaves (PREREG 96-98). If a bound is wanted, add a REPORTED (non-gating) receipt: the accumulate-arm's own erasure-loss magnitude and gradient-norm slope over [2,8], so a reader can see how much extra optimization pressure the dose axis carries independent of any capability claim.

findings.title: The guard-excluded estimator emits its own headline verdict but is never certified by the proposed gate
findings.claim: The spec certifies one estimator (raw gen_aggregate slope) while `compute_verdict` runs a SECOND estimator on the guard-excluded channel whose disagreement alone produces the distinct verdict string NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B -- an uncertified estimator emitting a headline, which is precisely the gap the estimator-admissibility rung exists to close.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:564 computes `dr_ge = dose_response(..., battery_key='battery_gen_guard_excl')`, and :574-578 route a raw-COUPLED result to `NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B` purely on `not dr_ge['coupled']`. The guard-excluded aggregate has a per-checkpoint VARYING denominator (guard-zeroed items removed, capability_battery_gen.py:309-317), so its lattice, its variance, and its arm-asymmetry differ from the raw channel -- the prereg already concedes a downward bias when the accumulate arm guards more (PREREG lines 156-159). A null pool built on `gen_aggregate` is therefore not exchangeable with the guard-excluded estimator's noise.
findings.fix: Run the estimator gate TWICE -- once per battery_key, using pseudo-deltas built from points[*].gen_aggregate and points[*].gen_aggregate_guard_excl respectively (both are already on the grounding surface, coupling_confirm_v4.py:192-193) -- and pre-commit that the style-downgrade branch is reachable only when the guard-excluded estimator passes its own gate; otherwise that branch VOIDs instead of emitting a headline.

findings.title: Receipt: the pool's fields are exchangeable-with-the-verdict on the statistic (verified sound), but the spec's receipt omits the numbers that would let a reader audit the null
findings.claim: The pseudo-delta as specified does reproduce the verdict's own statistic exactly, so the statistic-identity leg of S1 is sound -- but the receipt fields listed in S4 (pool size, injected slope, pseudo-run count) are insufficient to audit any of the exchangeability questions above.
findings.evidence: `CBG.paired_delta` rounds each arm's aggregate to 4dp before subtracting expressly so the delta equals the difference of the two `points[*].gen_aggregate` values (capability_battery_gen.py:389-398, coupling_confirm_v4.py:192), so building d_ij from gen_aggregate is the same arithmetic path the estimator uses -- no mismatch there. But S4 lists only pool size, injected slope and pseudo-run count; nothing in it exposes pool SD vs realized-delta SD (E4), the draw rule (E5), the borrowed schedule (E6), or the atom-at-zero mass that would reveal a degenerate pool (E1).
findings.fix: Add to the receipt: pseudo-slope SD and the realized paired-delta SD at each fitted rank with their ratio; the fraction of pseudo-seeds with exactly-zero slope; the draw rule name and the enumerated draw count; the borrowed-schedule seed per pseudo-seed; and the full sorted list of pseudo-slopes (20 numbers -- cheap, and it lets any reader recompute both rates by hand).

lens_reviews.summary: The named hazard in (a) is genuinely avoided: the proposed pool never detrends the estimator's own fit, and the pool statistic is byte-identical to the verdict's statistic (paired_delta is just the difference of two 4dp-rounded aggregates, capability_battery_gen.py:389-398 vs coupling_confirm_v4.py:192), and (c) is clean -- the rule's threshold is the prereg constant MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (:107), never derived from the pool. But (b) and (d) both land, hard, and they are fatal. Ordered pseudo-pairs make the pool EXACTLY antisymmetric (slope is linear in the deltas, coupling_confirm_v4.py:355-358), so the pool is exactly 50/50 in sign by construction; and OLS slope is exactly equivariant under the injection (slope(d + c*r) = slope(d) + c, verified to 1e-17). Together those two identities pin BOTH legs to constants: recovery_rate at an exactly-bar-sized injection is 0.500 for ANY realized dispersion (measured: 0.5002, 0.5010, 0.5000, 0.4979, 0.4979 across pool SDs spanning 0.0005 to 0.0208) against a 0.80 floor it can never reach, and false_recovery_rate is analytically capped at 0.0663 against a 0.15 cap it can never breach. The gate as specced does not read the data: it VOIDs the run deterministically unless the channel is frozen, in which case (zero dispersion) it returns recovery 1.0000 / false-recovery 0.0000 -- a perfect two-sided pass certifying a dead estimator. That is attempt-3's F1 reproduced one level up, which is the exact defect the spec exists to close.

findings.title: Sensitivity leg is unpassable by construction: recovery_rate = 0.500 exactly, independent of the data
findings.claim: Injecting an effect of exactly MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE against a rule whose bar is exactly MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE pins the per-pseudo-seed detection probability at exactly 0.5 for any non-degenerate pool, so recovery_rate <= 0.50 < ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR=0.80 and S2 VOIDs the run with VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive regardless of what the GPU produces.
findings.evidence: Two exact identities. (1) `_per_seed_slope` is np.polyfit (coupling_confirm_v4.py:355-358), linear in y, so slope(d + c*r) = slope(d) + c exactly -- measured residual 1.04e-17. The injection is therefore a pure translation: `s + bar >= bar` reduces to `s >= 0`. (2) S1's ORDERED pairs (i,j) and (j,i) give d_ji = -d_ij pointwise on a shared rank axis, so slope_ji = -slope_ij exactly; the pool of up to 20 is {+/-s_1..+/-s_10} and #{s>=0} = #{s<0} exactly. Simulation over pool SDs 0.0005/0.002/0.005/0.01/0.0208: frac(pool>=0) = 0.5000 in every case; recovery_rate = 0.5002/0.5010/0.5000/0.4979/0.4979. The sign-flip involution (i,j)->(j,i) is measure-preserving, so this survives without-replacement drawing too. Analytic ceilings at the majority rule with p=0.5: n_admissible=5 -> 0.5000, n=4 -> 0.3125, n=3 -> 0.5000 (MIN_ADMISSIBLE_SEEDS=3 at :122 admits all three). The ONLY way to clear 0.80 is an atom of EXACTLY-ZERO pool slopes of share >= 0.36 (solved: needs per-seed detect prob p=0.680), i.e. the leg passes only to the degree that cross-seed fixed-vs-fixed comparisons produce an identically flat fit -- a frozen channel. Quantization makes this reachable and misleading: gen_aggregate is k/48 at MIN_DISJOINT=3 subtasks x 16 items, and a +-1-item pool has a 20% zero atom giving recovery 0.6835 (still short, and elevated for the wrong reason).
findings.fix: Do not inject at the decision boundary. Pre-commit an injection MULTIPLE k > 1 of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE and gate recovery there; report the full recovery curve over k in {0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3} in the receipt. Measured viability at k=2: recovery 1.000 / 0.973 / 0.896 at pool SDs 0.0013 / 0.0120 / 0.0180 -- data-dependent and failure-reachable, which is what a gate has to be. k=1.5 already discriminates (1.000 / 0.896 / 0.594). Freeze k=2 and ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR=0.80 together, and add a --dry fixture asserting that a pool with SD >= 1.2x the bar FAILS the leg.

findings.title: Specificity leg is unfailable by construction: max attainable false_recovery is 0.0663 against a 0.15 cap
findings.claim: Because the ordered-pair pool is exactly sign-symmetric, the COUPLED rule's `zero seeds at or below -bar` veto fires at exactly the same rate as the `majority at or above +bar` clause, which caps false_recovery_rate at 0.0663 for EVERY possible pool. ESTIMATOR_FALSE_RECOVERY_MAX=0.15 can therefore never bind, and S3 is decorative -- S4's claim that 'both legs gate' is unearned.
findings.evidence: With q = P(s >= bar) = P(s <= -bar) (equal by the antisymmetry of S1's ordered pairs), false_recovery = sum_{k=3..5} C(5,k) q^k (1-2q)^(5-k); maximised over all q in [0,0.5] this is 0.0663 at q=0.350 -- computed exhaustively over a 5001-point grid. Monte-Carlo agrees: 0.0000 at pool SD <= 0.0208, 0.0626 at SD 0.05, 0.0483 at SD 0.2 (i.e. it is not even monotone in noise -- a wildly noisy estimator scores BETTER on this leg because the veto starts firing). Both a perfectly deaf estimator (SD 0, false_recovery 0.0000) and a hysterical one (SD 0.2 = 13x the bar, false_recovery 0.0483) pass comfortably. This is not the styxx.instrument_admissibility tautology -- the threshold is the prereg constant MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (:107), not pool-derived -- but the outcome is the same: a leg whose PASS is a theorem about the construction, not a fact about the estimator.
findings.fix: Set ESTIMATOR_FALSE_RECOVERY_MAX strictly inside the attainable band [0, 0.0663] -- 0.05 discriminates in the simulation (SD 0.0208 -> 0.0000 PASS, SD 0.05 -> 0.0626 FAIL). Better: replace the leg with the sufficient statistic it is a lossy function of -- gate directly on the null pool's dispersion relative to the bar, e.g. `fraction of pool slopes with |s| >= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE <= q_max`, which states plainly what is being certified and can fail. Either way, record in the receipt the analytically attainable range of the rate so a future reader can see the cap is not vacuous.

findings.title: The injection enters downstream of the measurement, so the gate can never certify instrument response -- and a frozen channel scores a perfect two-sided pass
findings.claim: S2 adds MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE * r to the already-computed deltas, not to the underlying capability. The gate therefore tests only OLS arithmetic against the pool's dispersion; nothing about the battery, the aggregate, or paired_delta is exercised. Since recovery is monotone DECREASING in pool dispersion and dispersion is minimised by a channel that does not move, the certificate is maximally reassuring exactly when the measurement is dead in the tested regime.
findings.evidence: Simulated zero-dispersion pool (every cross-seed fixed-vs-fixed slope exactly 0, i.e. the selected gen aggregate never differs between two fixed runs across ranks 4/6/8): recovery_rate = 1.0000, false_recovery_rate = 0.0000 -- a flawless two-sided ADMISSIBLE. The real dose_response on such a run returns all-zero deltas, all-zero slopes, `below` = all seeds, `bounded_null` True (:423-425) and ships NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B (:586-587) carrying an estimator certificate that reads 'recovery 1.00'. The upstream battery gate does not cover this: full degeneracy is caught (VOID_INSTRUMENT__unmeasurable, the :1040 dry case), but PARTIAL saturation is not -- admissibility_gate (:265-346) only proves not-deaf to the posctl arm's gross collapse, which its own scope string concedes (:125-128), while the accumulate-vs-fixed regime can sit frozen on a 1/48 quantization grid with every delta exactly 0. The gate would certify that frozen estimator as sensitive.
findings.fix: State the certificate's scope honestly and narrow it, or move the injection upstream. Minimum: rename the legs to what they measure (estimator NOISE relative to the bar) and add an explicit scope string mirroring NOT_DEAF_SCOPE (:125-128) -- 'this certifies estimator precision on the realized null ONLY; it does not certify that the generation channel responds to a capability change of any size.' Stronger, and cheap at zero GPU: add a resampling leg that injects at the ITEM level (flip a pre-committed number of the 48 selected battery items on the accumulate arm's checkpoints to produce a bar-sized aggregate trajectory, then re-run aggregate -> paired_delta -> _per_seed_slope), so the quantization and the aggregation are inside the loop being tested. That is the only version that answers 'can this estimator recover a bar-sized effect' rather than 'is OLS linear'.

findings.title: The null pool is arm-type-blind and mean-zero by construction, so a passing gate cannot be read as estimator unbiasedness
findings.claim: S1's pool differences two FIXED arms, so any bias specific to the accumulate-vs-fixed contrast (the arm-type nuisance the FI and echo machinery exists for) is structurally absent from the null, and the antisymmetric closure forces the pool mean to exactly 0. A zero-centred pool is then a theorem, not evidence.
findings.evidence: Sum over ordered pairs of slope_ij is identically 0 because slope_ji = -slope_ij (see T1). The real estimator at :398 contrasts arms with different erasure geometry -- the accumulate arm's subspace grows via orthonormal_union (:689, b2_coupling_dose.py:60-71) while the fixed arm re-fits a rank-2 gold_subspace each refit (:691, b2_subspace_erasure.py:47-67 stacks exactly 2 directions), so the arms differ in gradient magnitude and generation length in ways the fixed-vs-fixed pool never samples. The harness already concedes this channel exists: trained_fi_dose_graded (:491-524) and the guard-excluded subtractive (:564-569) are both there precisely to catch arm-type drift.
findings.fix: Add a receipt field and a scope string on the certificate stating that the pool is a SEED-noise null, not an ARM-type null, and that the gate does not and cannot certify the estimator unbiased for the accumulate-vs-fixed contrast. Cross-reference the FI-downgrade and echo-subtractive branches as the (separate) machinery covering arm-type bias, so no reader collapses the two claims.

findings.title: The guard-excluded estimator is a co-decider and sole decider of a favourable-direction verdict, and the gate never touches it
findings.claim: compute_verdict runs dose_response TWICE -- on battery_gen and battery_gen_guard_excl -- and NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B is returned purely on the guard-excluded estimator failing to reach the bar. S4's guarantee that 'no favourable branch survives a failed leg' is false: that branch rides an estimator with no leg at all.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:563-564 computes `dr` and `dr_ge`; :574-578 returns NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B whenever `dr['coupled']` and not `dr_ge['coupled']`. The guard-excluded channel is a strictly noisier statistic than the raw one -- capability_battery_gen.py:326-344 returns scores_guard_excl computed with guard-zeroed items EXCLUDED, so its denominator varies per checkpoint, which the re-panel already flagged (minor: 'make the F5 subtractive a true paired subset (or disclose the downward bias)'). A deaf-or-noisy dr_ge converts a real COUPLED into a 'no capability price' headline. S1 as written pools only points[*].gen_aggregate (:192), never gen_aggregate_guard_excl (:193).
findings.fix: Run the estimator gate on BOTH channels -- build a second pool from points[*].gen_aggregate_guard_excl and require both to pass, or explicitly demote the style-downgrade branch to PARTIAL when only the raw channel is certified. Add a --dry fixture in which dr is COUPLED, dr_ge is noise, and the run does NOT return NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B.

findings.title: Undefined rank axis and undefined None-slope handling reintroduce re-panel #6's reduced-denominator FATAL inside the gate
findings.claim: S1 says the rank axis is 'taken from the accumulate arm's matched-step schedule' without saying WHOSE -- erased_rank is seed-specific -- and S2/S3 never say what happens when a pseudo-seed yields a None slope. If None pseudo-seeds are dropped from the pseudo-run rather than counted as not-above-bar, the exact defect that killed the rebuild at panel #6 recurs one level up, inside the gate meant to certify it.
findings.evidence: erased_rank is recorded per checkpoint per arm per seed (:666, :188-196) and the accumulate arm's growth depends on orthonormal_union's tol-1e-6 rank test (b2_coupling_dose.py:69), so schedules diverge across seeds; the antisymmetry that T1 and T2 rest on is exact only under a SHARED axis. `_per_seed_slope` returns None on fewer than 2 distinct ranks (:357-358), and the span [2,8] with step-0 excluded leaves only ~3 fitted checkpoints (the --dry fixture's RANKS_ at :930 grows 2 per refit, giving fitted ranks {4,6,8}), so stalls are live, not hypothetical. The harness's own comment at :413-418 documents this as the re-panel FATAL and MIN_ADMISSIBLE_SEEDS at :122 as the guard.
findings.fix: Pre-commit a single canonical rank axis for the pool (the accumulate arm's schedule of the LOWER-indexed seed of the pair, or a pooled median schedule -- state which) and pre-commit that a None pseudo-seed slope counts AGAINST recovery and AGAINST false-recovery (never dropped from the pseudo-run denominator). Add a --dry fixture with stalled pseudo-seeds asserting recovery does not inflate.

findings.title: 20 pseudo-seeds are 5 trajectories in disguise; the reported rates have no honest denominator
findings.claim: The pool's 20 ordered pairs are generated from 5 fixed trajectories, each appearing in 8 pairs, so the pseudo-seeds are neither independent nor 20 in number; recovery_rate and false_recovery_rate presented as frequencies over pseudo-runs carry an effective sample size closer to 5 and no interval.
findings.evidence: S1 forms all ordered (i,j) over SEEDS = [0,1,2,3,4] (:90). Each pseudo-run of n_admissible=5 draws from 20 ordered pairs contains a mirrored pair (i,j) and (j,i) -- perfectly anti-correlated by T1's identity -- with probability 1 - C(10,5)*2^5/C(20,5) = 1 - 0.520 = 0.480, and such runs are structurally pushed away from COUPLED by the zero-negatives veto. Each pseudo-seed slope is itself a 3-point OLS fit on a statistic quantized at 1/48, so the pool's own dispersion estimate is coarse.
findings.fix: Report effective sample size and a bootstrap interval on both rates in the receipt, and pre-commit that a pseudo-run may not contain both orientations of the same unordered pair (or use only the 10 unordered pairs with a pre-committed orientation rule). Do not present the rates as if they were 20 independent replicates.

findings.title: A recovery certificate at k x bar does not license a bounded null at 1 x bar -- the verdict string must carry the certified level
findings.claim: Once T1's fix moves the injection to k > 1, the sensitivity leg certifies only that an effect of k x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE would have been recovered. NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B asserts no price above 1 x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, a strictly stronger claim than the gate earns. Which claim the estimator's silence produces is a property of the verdict mapping -- the arc's own rung.
findings.evidence: MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.0152 is the prereg minimum price of interest, 0.0909 / (8-2) (:107). The bounded-null branch (:586-587) is the branch S2 exists to protect, and it is the only favourable-direction reading reachable after the gate passes. FROZEN_VERDICTS (:130-144) hardcodes the string, and the re-panel already flagged '_MDE' as a misnomer for the min-price-of-interest.
findings.fix: Rename the bounded-null verdict to carry the certified injection level explicitly, e.g. NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_2X_MIN_PRICE__estimator_certified_1p5B, and add the certified level plus the measured recovery curve to the receipt beside it. Update FROZEN_VERDICTS and the prereg together, and add a --dry assertion that the string's numeric level matches the frozen injection constant.

lens_reviews.lens: power-feasibility (constants, achievable rates, pool resolution, threshold provenance)
lens_reviews.summary: The schedule question resolves cleanly: 13 checkpoints at ranks 2,4,...,24 (coupling_confirm_v4.py:680-698,727; rank at :666), of which only steps 25/50/75 (ranks {4,6,8}) fall in RANK_SPAN with step>0 -- 3 fitted points, 1 residual df, per-seed slope SE ~0.036 (the prereg's own number, PREREG:344-348). Given that, the spec's constants are not merely arbitrary, they are IMPOSSIBLE: because S2 injects an effect exactly EQUAL to the decision threshold, and the S1 ordered-pair pool is exactly antisymmetric, P(a pseudo-seed lands at/above the bar) is 0.5 by construction for ANY estimator and ANY noise level -- so recovery_rate is capped at 0.5 and lands at 0.238 at the realized SE. ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR=0.80 therefore fires VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive with probability 1; the gate as specified voids every possible run, which is exactly the "gate that voids everything is as useless as no gate" failure the mandate names. Two further defects survive the obvious repair: the ordered-pair pool mechanically suppresses false-recovery (48% of draws contain an antipodal pair that makes COUPLED unreachable), so S3 certifies "specific" for free; and neither leg ever measures the branch actually at risk -- P(bounded_null | true effect = bar) is 0.164 and is never gated. Lowering the floor would leave both live. The gate needs a different pool, a different injection point, and a third leg -- that is a redesign of S1-S4, not a constants patch.

findings.title: ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR=0.80 is mathematically unattainable; S2 VOIDs every possible run
findings.claim: Because S2 injects a slope exactly EQUAL to the decision threshold, recovery_rate is bounded above by 0.5 for any estimator, any noise level, and any realized data -- so a 0.80 floor fires VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive with probability 1 and no finding branch is ever reachable.
findings.evidence: The COUPLED rule tests `s >= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE` (coupling_confirm_v4.py:421) with a strict-majority requirement (:424). OLS is linear, so an injected linear effect gives s_pseudo = slope_null + BAR exactly; hence P(s_pseudo >= BAR) = P(slope_null >= 0). The S1 pool over ORDERED pairs (i,j) and (j,i) satisfies slope_null(d_ij) = -slope_null(d_ji), so the pool is exactly antisymmetric and P(slope_null >= 0) = 0.5 EXACTLY (not asymptotically). Drawing 5 of 20 with exactly 10 positive is hypergeometric and symmetric about 2.5, so P(#above >= 3) = 0.5 exactly; the zero-negative condition can only reduce it. Computed recovery vs sigma at injection = BAR: sigma=0.0005 -> 0.500; 0.010 -> 0.498; 0.020 -> 0.405; 0.036 -> 0.238; 0.060 -> 0.139. The design's own SE is 0.036 (PREREG_B2_coupling_twosided_2026_07_16.md:344-348: paired-delta SE 0.1021, sum of squared rank deviations 8 over ranks {4,6,8}, 0.1021/sqrt(8)=0.036), giving recovery = 0.2383. To clear 0.80 at sigma=0.036 the injection must be 0.0483 = 3.18 x BAR (a total span effect of 0.290 versus the 0.0909 minimum price of interest).
findings.fix: Stop injecting at the bar and demanding power at the bar -- no threshold rule achieves 80% power at its own threshold. Replace S2 with a RECOVERY CURVE: sweep injected slope, define ESTIMATOR_MDE80 = the smallest injected slope with recovery >= 0.80, and pre-commit a bound ESTIMATOR_MDE80 <= K x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE. If the realized MDE80 exceeds the bar (it will: ~3.2x), the bounded-null verdict string must be RESTATED at ESTIMATOR_MDE80, not at MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE -- i.e. the honest claim becomes 'no paired price above 0.29 over the span', which is true and earned, instead of 'above 0.0909', which is not.

findings.title: S1's ordered-pair pool is exactly antisymmetric, making the S3 specificity leg pass by construction
findings.claim: Building the null pool from ORDERED pairs guarantees that for every pseudo-seed with slope s there is one with slope -s, which mechanically suppresses false_recovery_rate and independently caps recovery_rate -- S3 then certifies the estimator 'specific' without evidence, the same tautology signature that voided the shipped certifier and killed attempt 3.
findings.evidence: S1 specifies ordered pairs (i,j), i != j, giving 20 pseudo-seeds from 5 real seeds, but only 10 distinct magnitudes. Drawing 5 of 20 without replacement: draws containing at least one antipodal pair = 1 - C(10,5)*2^5/C(20,5) = 1 - 8064/15504 = 0.480. In the S3 leg (injection 0), any drawn antipodal pair with |s| >= BAR puts one slope at/above +BAR and its twin at/below -BAR, so `neg_above` is non-empty (coupling_confirm_v4.py:422) and `coupled` is False (:424) REGARDLESS of the data -- false_recovery is suppressed on ~half of all draws by pool construction, not by the estimator being specific. Computed false_recovery at sigma=0.036 without accounting for this is already only 0.066 against a 0.15 ceiling; the artifact pushes it lower still. The same artifact hits S2: under injection at BAR, an antipodal pair (s,-s) becomes (s+BAR, -s+BAR) and exactly one of the two is >= BAR, so a draw containing p antipodal pairs has max #above = 5-p, further depressing recovery below the 0.5 cap. Both legs are biased in the same direction -- toward VOID and toward 'specific' -- which is precisely the 'a noise floor alone selects FOR deaf instruments' mechanism one level up the stack.
findings.fix: Draw pseudo-seeds from UNORDERED pairs (i<j, 10 units) with an independently randomized sign per draw, and forbid any pseudo-run from containing two pseudo-seeds that share a real seed (which caps a pseudo-run at floor(5/2)=2 units -- itself a disclosure that 5 seeds cannot support a 5-unit pseudo-run of independent contrasts). Report the antipodal/shared-seed contamination fraction of the realized draws in the receipt.

findings.title: Both legs gate P(COUPLED); neither bounds the branch actually at risk of being unearned
findings.claim: S2 measures P(COUPLED | injected effect) and S3 measures P(COUPLED | null), but the verdict at risk in this design is the BOUNDED NULL, and P(bounded_null | true effect = MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE) is 0.164 at the design's own SE and is never measured or gated -- so an estimator can pass both proposed legs and still emit a favourable-ish 'no paired price above MDE' verdict 16% of the time when a real bar-sized price exists.
findings.evidence: The bounded-null branch `NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B` is returned on `dr['bounded_null']` (coupling_confirm_v4.py:586-587), which is computed as a strict majority BELOW the bar in magnitude (:423,:425) and is entirely independent of the COUPLED condition the two proposed legs exercise. At sigma=0.036 with a true effect of exactly MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, the three branches partition as COUPLED 0.238 / bounded_null 0.164 / PARTIAL 0.598. S4 states 'EITHER failure VOIDs the entire run (no favourable branch survives a failed leg)' -- but a run that PASSES both legs and lands on the bounded null has had that branch's false rate bounded by nothing. The spec's own stated rationale ('a non-COUPLED finding from an estimator that cannot recover a bar-sized effect is uninformative') targets exactly this quantity and then does not measure it.
findings.fix: Add a third, mandatory leg computed from the SAME pseudo-runs at zero extra cost: FALSE-BOUNDED-NULL. On pseudo-runs injected at MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, compute false_bounded_null_rate = fraction returning the bounded-null branch (majority with |slope| < BAR), and require it <= a pre-committed ESTIMATOR_FALSE_NULL_MAX, else VOID_COUPLING__estimator_cannot_bound. At the current design this rate is 0.164 and would fail any sane ceiling -- which is the honest answer: the bounded null is not statable at 0.0152 with this n, only at ESTIMATOR_MDE80.

findings.title: Pseudo-runs are drawn at the wrong shape: n_admissible units, when the realized rule runs on n_slopes units against an n_admissible denominator
findings.claim: S2 draws pseudo-runs of n_admissible pseudo-seeds, but the frozen rule scores n_slopes DEFINED slopes against an n_admissible DENOMINATOR, so a run with stalled seeds is certified by a gate that exercised a strictly easier rule instance.
findings.evidence: The majority denominator is `n_admissible = len(admissible_seeds)` while the numerator counts only non-None slopes (coupling_confirm_v4.py:419-424), with the deliberate re-panel-#6 property that stalled seeds count AGAINST the majority; the underpowered guard only requires n_admissible_slopes >= 3 (:572). Pseudo-seeds never stall by construction (they are drawn from defined slopes), so a pseudo-run always has n_slopes == n_admissible. Computed recovery at injection = BAR, sigma=0.036: n_slopes=5 -> 0.238; n_slopes=4 -> 0.213; n_slopes=3 -> 0.125. Stalling is a live case -- the harness ships a dedicated stalled-seed fixture (`acc_arm_stalled`, :959-963) and `orthonormal_union` (b2_coupling_dose.py:60-71) can add fewer than 2 new directions per refit, with rank read as an int-truncated cross-layer mean (coupling_confirm_v4.py:666). A gate certifying 0.238 for a run that actually executes at 0.125 is certifying the wrong estimator.
findings.fix: Draw pseudo-runs matching the REALIZED (n_admissible, n_slopes) shape: sample n_slopes pseudo-seeds and pad with (n_admissible - n_slopes) None-slope stalled units, then apply the frozen rule verbatim. Record both counts in the receipt alongside both rates.

findings.title: The rank axis for a cross-seed pseudo-delta is undefined, and S1 has no minimum-pool guard
findings.claim: S1 assigns d_ij(step) to 'the rank axis r(step) taken from the accumulate arm's matched-step schedule' without naming WHICH seed's accumulate arm, and the schedules are not guaranteed identical across seeds; with no MIN_POOL constant the gate can also be computed on a degenerate pool with no unmeasurable branch to catch it.
findings.evidence: erased_rank is `int(sum(Uacc[L].shape[1] for L in SCAN) / len(SCAN))` (coupling_confirm_v4.py:666) -- an int-truncated mean over layers, where each layer independently gains 0-2 dims per refit via `orthonormal_union` (b2_coupling_dose.py:60-71, which drops any direction already in the span at tol=1e-6). Seed i and seed j can therefore be at different ranks at the same step, making r(step) for d_ij ambiguous; if seed i has stalled (its own accumulate arm never leaves rank 2 in-span) no valid axis exists at all. Every other gate in the harness carries an explicit unmeasurable floor -- MIN_POSITIVE_MARKED=6, MIN_DERIVE_NULLS=8, MIN_GATE_NULLS=8 (:118-120) routing to VOID_COUPLING__admissibility_unmeasurable (:319-321) -- and S1-S4 has no analogue.
findings.fix: Freeze the rule as: a step enters pseudo-seed (i,j) only if r_i(step) == r_j(step) and that common rank is in RANK_SPAN with step > 0; a pseudo-seed is dropped unless >= 2 distinct ranks survive. Add MIN_ESTIMATOR_POOL (pre-committed) and a VOID_COUPLING__estimator_unmeasurable branch, and report the realized pool size and per-pseudo-seed fitted-rank counts.

findings.title: The S1 null pool is not the estimator's own noise distribution, and the mismatch is one-directional
findings.claim: The real estimator is a WITHIN-seed contrast (fixed_i - accumulate_i) that differences out that seed's own fine-tuning trajectory, while the S1 pool is a CROSS-seed contrast (fixed_i - fixed_j) that carries seed-to-seed trajectory divergence the real statistic never sees -- inflating the pool's slope variance in exactly the direction that makes S2 harder to pass and S3 easier.
findings.evidence: The scored statistic is `CBG.paired_delta(fix_p, acc_p, selected)` at matched step within one seed (coupling_confirm_v4.py:398, capability_battery_gen.py:389-394), and the prereg names shared-drift cancellation as the whole point ('shared fine-tuning drift differences out; attempt 2's disease', PREREG:106-108). S1's pseudo-delta has no such cancellation. Direction of the bias: larger sigma lowers BOTH recovery (0.238 at sigma=0.036 -> 0.139 at 0.060) and false_recovery -- the same one-way push as PF-2, toward VOID plus a free 'specific' certification. The prereg already flags cross-seed exchangeability as an assumption rather than a theorem for the conformal threshold (PREREG:320-323); S1 inherits that assumption and adds a variance-inflation term on top.
findings.fix: Center each fixed arm's in-span values on its own mean before differencing (removes the seed-level offset while keeping trend), and add a mandatory calibration receipt: the ratio of pool slope SD to the realized within-seed paired-delta residual SD. If that ratio exceeds a pre-committed bound, the pool is disclosed as a conservative upper bound on the estimator's noise and the recovery number is labelled a LOWER bound, not the estimator's power.

findings.title: The pool cannot resolve a rate against a 0.15 or 0.80 bar; a binomial CI at the pseudo-run count would misreport the uncertainty
findings.claim: The entire S1 pool is a deterministic function of 15 scalars (5 seeds x 3 in-span fixed checkpoints) carrying at most 4 independent pairwise contrasts, so both rates have a real standard error near 0.18 against a 0.15 bar -- while the receipt's natural binomial CI over pseudo-runs shrinks like 1/sqrt(B) and will read +/-0.026, overstating confidence by an order of magnitude.
findings.evidence: In-span step>0 checkpoints are steps 25/50/75 only (schedule at coupling_confirm_v4.py:680-698 with STEPS=300, REFIT_EVERY=25 at :95-96; ranks confirmed by the harness's own fixture RANKS_=[2,4,...,20] at :930), so the fixed arm contributes exactly 3 in-span values per seed = 15 scalars total; the pairwise-difference space of 5 units has rank 4. Rate SE against a 0.15 bar: n_eff=4 -> 0.179 (95% CI +/-0.350); n_eff=5 -> 0.160; n_eff=20 (the nominal but dependent pseudo-seed count) -> 0.080. Monte-Carlo-only SE at B=1000 pseudo-runs and true rate 0.24 is 0.0135. The leg therefore cannot distinguish a false-recovery rate of 0.15 from one of 0.35. S1-S4 also never fixes B or states whether draws are with or without replacement -- leaving a knob settable at result time, the same defect panel #6 flagged on PAIR_DROP.
findings.fix: Pre-commit B and the draw scheme in the prereg. Report the rate with a leave-one-SEED-out jackknife interval (5 replicates) as the headline uncertainty, and label any binomial-over-B interval explicitly 'Monte Carlo resampling only, not estimation uncertainty'. If the jackknife interval straddles the bar, the leg is unmeasurable and must VOID rather than pass.

findings.title: 0.80 has no derivation, and its guaranteed failure creates the exact post-hoc-retune pressure that PAIR_DROP 0.15 already cost this arc
findings.claim: ESTIMATOR_FALSE_RECOVERY_MAX=0.15 at least inherits lineage from MAX_FIRE, but ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR=0.80 is asserted with no derivation -- and since it is provably unmeetable, freezing it guarantees that the first real run VOIDs and the constant gets lowered with the data in hand.
findings.evidence: MAX_FIRE = 0.15 is already the harness's specificity constant (coupling_confirm_v4.py:116) with provenance in PREREG:299-323, so the S3 bar has a defensible parent. Nothing in the spec or prereg derives 0.80; the nearest relative is the 80%-power convention used for the AUROC floor arithmetic (PREREG:330-336), which is a different quantity on a different statistic. Critically, the achievable recovery rate is fully computable at ZERO GPU cost before freeze -- it is a closed-form function of the per-seed slope SE (0.036, PREREG:348), the rank grid ({4,6,8}), and the frozen rule (coupling_confirm_v4.py:421-424) -- so there is no excuse for freezing a value whose feasibility was never checked. Panel #6's live THRESHOLD PROVENANCE major is exactly this failure one iteration earlier.
findings.fix: Do the recovery arithmetic NOW, pre-freeze, and write the derivation into the prereg with the resulting number -- including the plain statement that at 5 seeds, 3 fitted ranks, and a bar set at the minimum price of interest, the design's power to return COUPLED at a true bar-sized effect is 0.238. If that number is judged too low, the correct response is to change the DESIGN (more seeds, or REFIT_EVERY reduced so more distinct ranks land inside [2,8], or a larger battery to cut the 0.0722 per-checkpoint SE), never to move the floor after seeing the run.

findings.title: The estimator gate is computed on the same population it gates, and the correlation runs favourable
findings.claim: All 5 fixed-arm trajectories feed BOTH the S1 null pool and the real paired baseline, so the gate shares its population with the estimator it certifies -- violating the prereg's own disjointness law -- and the shared component correlates gate passage with COUPLED reachability in the favourable direction.
findings.evidence: The scored delta is fixed_i - accumulate_i (coupling_confirm_v4.py:398) and S1's pool is fixed_i - fixed_j over the same seeds and the same in-span steps. The prereg's stated rail is that 'no threshold here ever touches the population it gates' (coupling_confirm_v4.py:12-15), implemented for the battery gate as DERIVE_SEEDS={0,1} disjoint from GATE_SEEDS={2,3,4} (:91-92, PREREG:310-312) precisely because the private-13 retro-cert VOIDed NONSPECIFIC on threshold transfer (PREREG:300-306). The estimator gate has no such split and cannot get one at 5 seeds. Direction of the leak: a run whose fixed arms happened to be quiet yields small null slopes (fewer negatives -> higher recovery -> S2 passes) AND a quieter real paired delta (-> COUPLED more reachable). The gate is most likely to pass exactly on the runs most likely to return the program-favourable verdict.
findings.fix: Either derive the pool from the DERIVE_SEEDS fixed arms only and restrict the scored estimator to GATE_SEEDS (which drops the finding to 3 seeds and should be costed honestly), or -- if the population must be shared -- state the overlap and its favourable direction explicitly in the prereg and the receipt, and report the correlation between each seed's own null-pool slope magnitude and its realized paired-delta slope so the leak is auditable rather than assumed absent.

findings.title: The two new VOID strings are absent from FROZEN_VERDICTS, which the --dry hygiene check asserts over
findings.claim: S2 and S3 introduce VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive and VOID_COUPLING__estimator_nonspecific, but neither appears in the frozen verdict list, so the harness's own verdict-set assertions would not cover them.
findings.evidence: FROZEN_VERDICTS is defined at coupling_confirm_v4.py:130-144 and enumerated in PREREG:380-392 as the complete decision order; the --dry hygiene checks scan that list for break-claim substrings and assert every dry verdict is a member (:1246-1252). Adding gate branches without extending the list leaves the new strings unasserted by both the code-level check and the prereg's decision-order table.
findings.fix: Add both strings (plus VOID_COUPLING__estimator_unmeasurable and any false-bounded-null string from PF-3) to FROZEN_VERDICTS, extend the prereg decision order to place the estimator gate explicitly after the battery gate and before the finding branches, and add --dry fixtures driving each new branch.

refutation_rulings.reasoning:
NOT REFUTED. Every factual assertion in the finding is verified against the code.

CONFIRMED IN CODE:
1. coupling_confirm_v4.py:563-564 computes two estimators via the same dose_response function with different battery_key ("battery_gen" and "battery_gen_guard_excl").
2. coupling_confirm_v4.py:574-578 -- the NO_CAPABILITY_PRICE__style_downgraded_1p5B branch is decided SOLELY by `not dr_ge["coupled"]`. It fires on the second estimator's SILENCE, never on a positive dr_ge finding.
3. admissibility_gate (coupling_confirm_v4.py:277-283, :300-303) reads p["gen_aggregate"] exclusively. Nothing in the frozen order gates the guard-excluded channel -- not the battery gate, not guard_fire_totals (:200-214, reported non-gating at :902), and not trained_fi_dose_graded (:581, which is only reachable AFTER dr_ge["coupled"] is True and therefore cannot cover the deaf case).
4. The bias direction is not speculative. capability_battery_gen.py:319-321: n_scored = n - (rep + echo); acc_guard_excl = correct / n_scored. `correct` is unchanged and only the denominator shrinks, so acc_guard_excl >= acc always, and the inflation is larger on whichever arm guards more. The accumulate arm guards more as it degrades, so the guard-excluded paired delta shrinks and the slope is biased DOWNWARD -- toward not-COUPLED, i.e. toward firing the branch. PREREG_B2:156-159 concedes exactly this in writing.
5. The fix is computable at result time with zero GPU: points already carries gen_aggregate_guard_excl (coupling_confirm_v4.py:193), MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE is channel-independent (:421-424), and the same _per_seed_slope path serves both channels.

THE SPEC AS WRITTEN: S1 says verbatim "d_ij(step) = fixed_i.gen_aggregate(step) - fixed_j.gen_aggregate(step)". Nothing in S1-S4 mentions the guard-excluded field or the dr_ge estimator. So after the spec ships, the pipeline still contains one estimator that is uncertified in BOTH directions and whose deafness alone emits a frozen verdict string. S4's own guarantee -- "the gate cannot select for a deaf estimator" -- is therefore false of the pipeline it is wired into.

KILL ATTEMPTS THAT FAILED:
(a) "A deaf dr_ge is conservative -- it only removes COUPLED, never creates it." This trims the finding's framing (the defect cannot manufacture the paper-restarting COUPLED branch) but does not refute it, because the fall-through is NOT a VOID. It is frozen verdict #13, an affirmative attribution that PREREG_B2:156 itself markets as "a stronger, more precise statement than the bounded null." PREREG_B2:30-32 records that attempt 3 was killed for exactly this: validating in one direction and mapping silence onto a headline claim, because a floor alone selects FOR deaf instruments. A one-directional safety argument is the program's twice-fatal error, reproduced here on a second channel.
(b) "Handled elsewhere in the frozen order." Traced -- nothing gates dr_ge. Worse, the branch is inert when guards rarely fire (the two aggregates are then identical, dr_ge == dr) and only bites when guards fire heavily, which is precisely the regime with the smallest n_scored, the coarsest quantization, and the largest downward bias. The risk is concentrated in the estimator's worst regime.
(c) "MAJOR dressed as fatal -- the fix is a loop extension." The cheapness of the remedy is real and worth recording (no redesign; extend S1-S4 over battery_key plus one new VOID string), but it does not bear on whether an unearned affirmative verdict is reachable. It is.

ONE AMENDMENT THAT STRENGTHENS THE FIX: the finding emphasizes the sensitivity leg, but the SPECIFICITY leg is also required on the guard-excluded channel. A nonspecific dr_ge would spuriously read COUPLED and wave a raw COUPLED through the subtractive check unearned -- that failure runs in the program-favourable direction. Both legs, both channels, both rates side by side in the receipt, as the proposed fix states.

Severity FATAL rather than MAJOR: the defect reaches an affirmative, pre-committed verdict string that the prereg elevates above the bounded null, sourced entirely from an estimator whose sensitivity is never measured, in the exact failure mode the prereg's stated law exists to forbid.

REFUTED -- accurate observation, wrong severity. The harm mechanism is blocked by arithmetic in the spec itself.

WHAT THE CLAIM GETS RIGHT (code-confirmed, not disputed):
- The real statistic is a within-seed, cross-arm contrast: `coupling_confirm_v4.py:398` `d = CBG.paired_delta(fix_p[battery_key], acc_p[battery_key], selected)`, and `capability_battery_gen.py:389-398` confirms it is exactly `round(agg_fixed,4) - round(agg_acc,4)`.
- The arms are asymmetric: `coupling_confirm_v4.py:689` accumulate takes `B2COUP.orthonormal_union(Uacc[L], subs_now[L])` (rank climbs), `:692` fixed does `Uacc[L] = subs_now[L]` (rank re-pinned every refit). The dose axis lives only on the accumulate arm.
- S1's pool is therefore a cross-seed, within-fixed-arm contrast containing zero accumulate-arm variance. The spec's phrase "EXACTLY the population and axis the real estimator fits" is an overclaim about ARM COMPOSITION (it is exact only as to the step>0 / rank-span filter). That much should be corrected in the spec text.

WHY IT IS NOT FATAL:

(1) The direction of the mismatch is asserted, not shown -- the claim concedes this ("the direction of the mismatch is unverified"), and there is a structural term pushing the OTHER way that the claim omits. The real paired delta is built to exploit within-seed covariance: `:623` `torch.manual_seed(seed); np.random.seed(seed)`, `:634` `rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)`, and the first refit is at step 25 (`:683`), so the fixed and accumulate arms of a seed share base weights, LoRA init, and the entire batch stream, and are byte-identical trajectories until step 25. Cov(A,F) > 0 is structural, and Var(real) = Var(A) + Var(F) - 2Cov. The pseudo-pool draws two INDEPENDENT seeds, so Var(pseudo) = 2Var(F) with NO covariance cancellation. The claim's missing-Var(A) term and this lost-2Cov term have opposite signs and neither is quantified. That is a MAJOR-grade unknown, not a demonstrated fatal.

(2) The claimed harm path cannot execute. S2 injects an effect of EXACTLY `MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE` and the frozen COUPLED rule (`:424` `len(pos_above) > n_admissible / 2 and len(neg_above) == 0`, with `pos_above` = slopes `>= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE` at `:421`) tests at/above that same bar. The S1 pool over ORDERED pairs (i,j) and (j,i) contains d_ji = -d_ij, so the multiset of pseudo-slopes is exactly symmetric about 0. Hence P(injected pseudo-seed lands at/above the bar) = P(pool slope >= 0) = 0.5 -- INDEPENDENT of the pool's SD. The pool's noise level cancels out of the sensitivity leg entirely. So recovery_rate <= P(Binom(n, 0.5) > n/2) = 0.5 for odd n, times P(no seed at/below -bar) <= 1. A pool that is quieter than the real estimator does not raise recovery_rate above 0.5; it pushes it TOWARD 0.5 from below by making the "zero negatives" clause easier. Against the proposed floor of 0.80, the gate VOIDs with `VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive`. The claimed sequence "recovery_rate OVERSTATES power -> the gate passes -> NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B is stamped estimator-validated" is unreachable. The error direction of an under-dispersed pool here is conservative (VOID), which is the direction the program pre-accepts.

(3) The one residual path is a degeneracy the claim does not raise or quantify: if the quiescent fixed arm produces pseudo-slopes of EXACTLY 0 (all three fitted pseudo-deltas equal), those draws land at the bar and count as above it. To lift per-seed p from 0.50 to the ~0.72 needed for recovery_rate 0.80 requires a tie mass of ~44% -- roughly 9 of the 20 ordered pseudo-pairs with an identically flat fit across ranks {4,6,8}. Aggregates move on a 1/48 lattice per sub-task and seeds differ in LoRA init and batch order, so this is a corner case, not the default. And S4 already mandates reporting both rates and the pool size, so the signature of that corner (recovery 1.00 / false_recovery 0.00 / tiny pool dispersion) lands on the receipt where a panel reads it.

VERDICT: MAJOR. Adopt the cheap half of the proposed remedy -- report the pseudo-pool's residual SD alongside the real per-seed fit residual SD in the receipt, and fix the spec's "EXACTLY the population" wording to disclose that the pool substitutes fixed_j for the accumulate arm. Do NOT adopt the F2-style "detrend the real paired deltas" remedy on this finding's authority: the fit is 3 points per seed (prereg lines 346-348, realized ranks {4,6,8}), so detrended real residuals carry 1 df per seed / 5 df total versus 20 pseudo-seeds, and that trade is not obviously favourable. Also do NOT stamp `VOID_COUPLING__estimator_null_understates_noise` as proposed -- on the analysis above an under-dispersed pool already fails the sensitivity leg, so that void string is redundant with `estimator_insensitive`.

SEPARATE ISSUE THE PANEL SHOULD OPEN (not this finding, but it surfaced from the same arithmetic and is load-bearing): because injection is at exactly the bar and the pool is exactly symmetric, ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR = 0.80 is mathematically unreachable at any pool dispersion (ceiling 0.5). As written, S2 voids every run. That needs a fix -- inject above the bar (e.g. 2x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE) or set the floor beneath 0.5 -- before the spec can be frozen.
